# CheckStatus { #pycmdcheck.CheckStatus }

```python
CheckStatus()
```

Status of a check result.

Each check returns one of these statuses to indicate
whether the check passed, failed, or encountered issues.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**OK**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Check passed successfully.

<code>[**NOTE**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Informational message, not a problem.

<code>[**WARNING**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Potential issue that should be reviewed.

<code>[**ERROR**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Check failed.

<code>[**SKIPPED**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Check was skipped (e.g., required tool not installed).

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> status = CheckStatus.OK
>>> status.symbol
'✓'
>>> status.color
'green'
>>> str(status)
'ok'
```
